summary: The dataset/protocol backbone is complete enough for the other Codex terminal to continue OCR ingestion against a stable authoring/runtime contract.

completed.notes: Defines authoring/runtime split, provenance rules, normalization rules, and the rule that secondary agents may update authoring data but must not write runtime JSON directly.
Formal contract for the current P0 dataset layers.
Includes the current OCR-complete Xanathar entry and 9 pending OCR PDFs.
Validates authoring JSON, cross-references, skills, state ids, and writes runtime-safe JSON into data/runtime/.
Prints the remaining pending OCR jobs and recommended Azure commands.
The vertical slice now loads normalized JSON rather than hardcoded encounter dictionaries.

not_completed_here.notes: I did not run the rest of the Azure OCR jobs here. The other Codex terminal already has the operational OCR system for that.
After each PDF is OCR-complete, the secondary agent should update source_documents and then begin authoring structured content with source_refs.

required_workflow_for_other_terminal: Run scripts/list_pending_ocr.py to enumerate pending PDFs.
Process PDFs with the existing Azure OCR pipeline.
Update data/authoring/source_documents.json entries from pending_ocr to ocr_complete and fill the OCR artifact paths.
Run python3 scripts/validate_content.py after source_documents changes.
Do not write or hand-edit data/runtime files directly; always regenerate them through validate_content.py.
